column1: # Typed range input during POINT mode (SPEC §9.4).
# In POINT for any range-asking command, the user can type a
# Lotus-style range address (e.g. `c8..d12`) and press Enter to
# commit it, replacing the auto-painted highlight.
# Seed values so format changes are visible.
# Path 1: typed range commits the format command.
# Path 2: reverse corners normalize. Commands run without panic.
# Path 3: Esc with non-empty typed buffer clears the buffer and
# returns to highlight POINT (does NOT cancel back to READY).
# Path 4: Backspace edits the typed buffer.
# Path 5: bad input on Enter is a silent no-op — stays in POINT,
# buffer is cleared so the user can retype.
# Recover by typing a valid address.
# Path 6: lowercase letters are accepted (parser uppercases).
# Path 7: existing pointer-highlight POINT (no typed buffer)
# behaves exactly as before — arrows paint, Enter commits.
# Path 8: '.' as the first POINT keystroke still toggles the anchor
# (not appended to the typed buffer). Anchor toggle in single-cell
# state moves the anchor onto the pointer — observable by then
# moving and seeing the highlight grow from A1.
